text: Whatever you can't accept, you can let go of. Whatever you can let go of, you can have.
author: Hale Dwoskin

text: Emotions are just emotions. They are not you, they are not facts, and you can let them go.
author: Hale Dwoskin

text: Resistance is a feeling of moving forward with the brakes on. When you let go, you feel more and more in charge and less and less a victim of whatever's happening.
author: Hale Dwoskin

text: Pain is a label. The suffering comes from the label, not from the sensation itself.
author: Hale Dwoskin

text: At your core, you are already whole, already complete, already enough as you are.
author: Hale Dwoskin

text: Every time you release, it's just a decision, a simple choice. You have that choice to make in every moment of every day.
author: Hale Dwoskin

text: When you let go, your mind quiets. You have more ability to focus, to concentrate, to follow through, to get out of your own way.
author: Hale Dwoskin

text: Just trying to hold in mind the 'positive' does not work unless you let go of the 'negative' — the past, your excess baggage.
author: Hale Dwoskin

text: Guilt is an unconscious 'I owe you' for punishment.
author: Hale Dwoskin

text: When we resist something we don't want, we are also resisting what we do want. Resistance is resistance either way, and that is what's blocking the flow.
author: Hale Dwoskin

text: Say 'I feel angry' or 'I feel sad,' which is more valid than 'I am angry' or 'I am sad.' You validate your experience without invalidating who you are.
author: Hale Dwoskin

text: Any feeling that comes up is just a feeling. It's not you.
author: Hale Dwoskin

text: When we want to change something, we're resisting it. Ironically, it's that very resistance that holds it in place.
author: Hale Dwoskin

text: You can choose to be right, or you can choose to be free.
author: Hale Dwoskin

text: As you let go of the wanting to control, you actually feel more in control.
author: Hale Dwoskin

text: Your mind is like a computer. Releasing is the ultimate delete button for your mental viruses.
author: Hale Dwoskin

text: Awareness is primary, everything else that we experience is secondary.
author: Hale Dwoskin

text: We are already that which we seek. We just have to let go of the belief that we aren't.
author: Hale Dwoskin

text: The willingness to let go of wanting to figure it out allows the answer to just be there.
author: Hale Dwoskin

text: Desire is a lack. Whenever we desire something, we are saying we don't have it.
author: Hale Dwoskin

text: Your freedom is already here and now.
author: Hale Dwoskin

text: Stop rushing past life. Begin to approach your life as though you have all the time in the world. The moment that offers the greatest opportunity for self-discovery is now.
author: Hale Dwoskin

text: One of the main ways that we create disappointments and unhappiness is by holding onto limiting thoughts and feelings.
author: Hale Dwoskin

text: You are the key to your own happiness, health, well-being, and success. All you need to do is use this key to unlock the secrets of freedom and happiness within your own heart.
author: Hale Dwoskin

text: Letting go is natural. Surrender is natural. Holding on is unnatural.
author: Hale Dwoskin

text: At your core, there is this self-radiant awareness that is your true nature. At our core, there is no difference at all between what you are and what I am.
author: Hale Dwoskin

text: It's not the struggle that leads to manifesting the goal. It's the letting go of the struggle that makes it possible.
author: Hale Dwoskin

text: When you recognize the true source of who you are, that brings a whole other level of freedom from suffering and unshakable happiness.
author: Hale Dwoskin

text: Suppression is keeping a lid on our emotions, pushing them back down, denying them, repressing them and pretending that they don't exist.
author: Hale Dwoskin

text: The people that really make a change are the people that move up into courage or acceptance.
author: Hale Dwoskin

text: Release your wanting and allow yourself to have.
author: Hale Dwoskin

text: If you feel stuck, either let go of wanting to change it or allow yourself to feel the way you do. Either one will help dissolve the feeling of stuckness.
author: Hale Dwoskin

text: If you dive below the waves on the surface of consciousness, there is always calm.
author: Hale Dwoskin

text: Instead of wanting to be loved, allow yourself to recognize that you are love.
author: Hale Dwoskin

text: Let go of wanting to make your problems real again.
author: Hale Dwoskin

text: Live the happiness and be the happiness.
author: Hale Dwoskin

text: The presence of awareness that you are is pure welcoming. Without words, it says yes to everything.
author: Hale Dwoskin

text: Welcome your thoughts, your sensations, your feelings, and the stories that you tell yourself. Just allow them all to be here, and know that everything is okay as it is.
author: Hale Dwoskin

text: You are already unlimited Beingness. Every path or technique is designed only to make you self-obvious to yourself.
author: Hale Dwoskin

text: Your mind can be your worst master, but a great servant.
author: Hale Dwoskin

text: You can't change an experience. But you can change the way you react to it.
author: Hale Dwoskin

text: Anything we focus on we do create. So if we're really angry at a war that's going on, or strife, or suffering, we're adding our energy to it.
author: Hale Dwoskin

text: When we resist the things in our life, what we resist persists. Anything in your mental state that you resist, persists.
author: Hale Dwoskin

text: Without Awareness there is no experiencing. We get so caught up in changes in the body-mind-world that we miss the natural beauty and bliss of pure being.
author: Hale Dwoskin

text: Allow yourself to explore the possibility that there is nothing separate from the Truth of who you are.
author: Hale Dwoskin

text: The Beingness-Awareness that you are is always the most intimate part of every experience.
author: Hale Dwoskin

text: You can have anything you want, providing you first let go of wanting it.
author: Lester Levenson

text: The mind will never discover who you are because the mind is the cover-up of who you are. It's only by letting go of the mind that you will discover who you are.
author: Lester Levenson

text: Instead of holding it down, you allow it up and out. Every suppressed feeling is pushing to get up and out all the time.
author: Lester Levenson

text: All unhappiness is caused by our trying to be limited, to be an ego. The more we are our Self, the happier we are.
author: Lester Levenson

text: When there's no effort, there's no ego. The more the effort the more the ego.
author: Lester Levenson

text: Every problem is an ego problem. In order to have a problem there has to be an ego-frustration.
author: Lester Levenson

text: Once you realize how effortless the highest way of life is, it takes tremendous effort to assume the opposite.
author: Lester Levenson

text: When one really loves, one can never be hurt.
author: Lester Levenson

text: The only method of receiving love is to give love, because what we give out must come back.
author: Lester Levenson

text: Human love is needing the other one. Divine love is giving to the other one.
author: Lester Levenson

text: Happiness equates to one's capacity to love rather than to being loved.
author: Lester Levenson

text: Love is a feeling of givingness with no thought of receiving any return for it.
author: Lester Levenson

text: Whatever happens to us originates in the mind.
author: Lester Levenson

text: Our real nature, the infinite real self that we are, is simply us minus the mind.
author: Lester Levenson

text: The mind is the only limitation you have.
author: Lester Levenson

text: To completely release the ego is to be completely free.
author: Lester Levenson

text: A feeling is just a program running in the mind. You can choose to stop the program.
author: Lester Levenson

text: Every physical problem has a mental cause. Clear the mind, and the body will follow.
author: Lester Levenson

text: Truth is always simple. It is the ego that complicates everything.
author: Lester Levenson

text: If you don't undo your mind and make it quieter, then your mind is becoming noisier. Therefore, you must continue to release your mind until you reach the end.
author: Lester Levenson

text: We can have peace if we let go of wanting to change the past and wanting to control the future.
author: Lester Levenson

text: Misery is complexity. Happiness is simplicity.
author: Lester Levenson

text: Every time you feel miserable, there is present an excellent opportunity to make a big step forward.
author: Lester Levenson

text: Release and allow yourself to see the perfection where the seeming imperfection seems to be.
author: Lester Levenson

text: The best measurement of our intelligence is our ability to be happy.
author: Lester Levenson

text: If we could only be, just be, we could see our infinity. We could see that we are the all.
author: Lester Levenson

text: If we don't like what's happening to us in the world, all we have to do is change our consciousness — and the world out there changes for us.
author: Lester Levenson

text: Feeling sad about anything is holding onto it. Say, 'This is something I have to let go of,' and immediately you will feel better.
author: Lester Levenson

text: The way to infinite joy is through the elimination of desire — no attachments, no aversions.
author: Lester Levenson

text: The original state for all Beings is Love. Our troubles are due only to our covering over this natural state.
author: Lester Levenson

text: Truth can be found by a process of eliminating wrong thoughts, which are thoughts of limitation. When all thoughts of limitation are eliminated, what remains is our natural, unlimited Self.
author: Lester Levenson

text: Peace is non-resistance, complete acceptance, identification with all, everyone, everything.
author: Lester Levenson

text: The vast majority of people's concept of love is actually hate, more or less. 'I need you, I must have you, I can't live without you, you're mine' — is all non-love.
author: Lester Levenson

text: Love eliminates fear. Love eliminates anxiety.
author: Lester Levenson

text: Growth is transcending yourself, your habitual self, which is none other than ego.
author: Lester Levenson

text: Don't probe darkness to understand light. Don't dwell on sickness to be healthy. Don't indulge in thoughts of lack to have supply.
author: Lester Levenson

text: There is more pain from holding on to the thought of pain than there is in the situation itself.
author: Lester Levenson

text: A mental picturing of that which we want, with the complete acceptance and the conviction that it is ours now, will bring it quickly.
author: Lester Levenson

text: When we take one step toward the Self, It takes nine steps towards us.
author: Lester Levenson

text: The whole object on the path is to let go of the ego. What remains is your Self.
author: Lester Levenson

text: Parliaments cannot right the world; but enough individuals feeling love can.
author: Lester Levenson

text: People need each other and think it is love. There's no hanging on to, or fencing in, of the other one when one loves.
author: Lester Levenson

text: There's nothing bad; there's just making errors on the way back Home.
author: Lester Levenson

text: The more miserable you get, the less you should look for an escape. Rather, isolate until you see and let go of the reason for it.
author: Lester Levenson

text: I was letting go and undoing the hell I had created. By squaring all with love, trying to love rather than trying to be loved, I became freer and freer, happier and happier.
author: Lester Levenson

text: The ego is the source of birth and death and when the ego is let go of, you die to death.
author: Lester Levenson

text: Attain to the place where no one and no thing can disturb you.
author: Lester Levenson

text: We are all Gods acting like goddamned fools.
author: Lester Levenson

text: What is joy? Joy is nothing but the Self. When desires are fulfilled, the mind is stilled. This allows the Self to shine forth — and that is what we call joy.
author: Lester Levenson

text: The happiness gets more and more intense the more we move toward total freedom. You don't disappear. You don't lose anything. You got more.
author: Lester Levenson

text: The facade is the ego. It is motivated by our seeking love. The only reward is frustration, as it is only by loving that one finds love.
author: Lester Levenson

text: Some of us are seeking happiness where it is and as a result are becoming happier. And others are seeking it blindly in the world where it is not and are becoming more frustrated.
author: Lester Levenson

text: The greatest happiness we can give to a mate comes from helping him or her to more understanding of Truth.
author: Lester Levenson

text: There's no such thing as a mistake — we do it! There's no such thing as an accident — we do it!
author: Lester Levenson

text: If you see misery, it's your misery. When you see the perfection where the seeming imperfection seems to be, the misery is only an apparency.
author: Lester Levenson

text: Letting go is like the sudden cessation of an inner pressure or the dropping of a weight. It is accompanied by a sudden feeling of relief and lightness, with an increased happiness and freedom.
author: David R. Hawkins
source: Letting Go: The Pathway of Surrender

text: Letting go involves being aware of a feeling, letting it come up, staying with it, and letting it run its course without wanting to make it different or do anything about it.
author: David R. Hawkins
source: Letting Go: The Pathway of Surrender

text: When letting go, ignore all thoughts. Focus on the feeling itself, not on the thoughts.
author: David R. Hawkins
source: Letting Go: The Pathway of Surrender

text: It is not thoughts or facts that are painful but the feelings that accompany them. Thoughts in and of themselves are painless, but not the feelings that underlie them.
author: David R. Hawkins
source: Letting Go: The Pathway of Surrender

text: The crucial point is: by changing ourselves, we change the world. As we become more loving on the inside, healing occurs on the outside.
author: David R. Hawkins
source: Letting Go: The Pathway of Surrender

text: We have three major ways of handling feelings: suppression, expression, and escape.
author: David R. Hawkins
source: Letting Go: The Pathway of Surrender

text: The suppressed and repressed feelings seek an outlet and utilize the events as triggers and excuses to vent themselves. We are like pressure-cookers ready to release steam when the opportunity arises.
author: David R. Hawkins
source: Letting Go: The Pathway of Surrender

text: What we are holding inside colors our world. If we let go of guilt, we will see innocence.
author: David R. Hawkins
source: Letting Go: The Pathway of Surrender

text: The world can only see us as we see ourselves.
author: David R. Hawkins
source: Letting Go: The Pathway of Surrender

text: To be surrendered means to have no strong emotion about a thing: 'It's okay if it happens, and it's okay if it doesn't.'
author: David R. Hawkins
source: Letting Go: The Pathway of Surrender

text: Feelings come and go, and eventually you realize that you are not your feelings, but that the real 'you' is merely witnessing them.
author: David R. Hawkins
source: Letting Go: The Pathway of Surrender

text: Simple kindness to one's self and all that lives is the most powerful transformational force of all.
author: David R. Hawkins
source: Letting Go: The Pathway of Surrender

text: Life events are opportunities to grow, expand, experience, and develop.
author: David R. Hawkins
source: Letting Go: The Pathway of Surrender

text: Inside of us, but out of awareness, is the truth that 'I already know everything I need to know.'
author: David R. Hawkins
source: Letting Go: The Pathway of Surrender

text: Force can bring satisfaction, but only power brings joy. Victory over others brings us satisfaction, but victory over ourselves brings us joy.
author: David R. Hawkins

text: Sometimes letting things go is an act of far greater power than defending or hanging on.
author: Eckhart Tolle

text: Surrender is the simple but profound wisdom of yielding to rather than opposing the flow of life.
author: Eckhart Tolle

text: Surrender to what is. Say 'yes' to life — and see how life suddenly starts working for you rather than against you.
author: Eckhart Tolle

text: Sometimes surrender means giving up trying to understand, and becoming comfortable with not knowing.
author: Eckhart Tolle

text: When you let go of the belief that you should or need to know who you are, what happens to confusion? Suddenly it is gone.
author: Eckhart Tolle

text: True freedom comes from releasing the resistance to what is.
author: Eckhart Tolle

text: Surrender is the inner transition from resistance to acceptance, from no to yes.
author: Eckhart Tolle

text: Attachment to things drops away by itself when you no longer seek to find yourself in them.
author: Eckhart Tolle

text: Nothing that comes and goes is you. 'I am bored.' Who knows this?
author: Eckhart Tolle

text: You come into the natural state by letting go of control, by letting go of effort and resting in a state of vividness.
author: Adyashanti

text: To let go is to release the images and emotions, the grudges and fears, the clingings and disappointments of the past that bind our spirit.
author: Jack Kornfield

text: To let go does not mean to get rid of. To let go means to let be. When we let be with compassion, things come and go on their own.
author: Jack Kornfield

text: Let go of the battle. Breathe quietly and let it be. Let your body relax and your heart soften.
author: Jack Kornfield

text: By letting go, we allow everything to come to us with grace.
author: Jack Kornfield

text: When we resist change, it's called suffering. But when we can completely let go and not struggle against it, when we can embrace the groundlessness of our situation and relax into its dynamic quality, that's called enlightenment.
author: Pema Chödrön
source: Living Beautifully with Uncertainty and Change

text: The essence of generosity is letting go. Pain is always a sign that we are holding on to something — usually ourselves.
author: Pema Chödrön

text: To be fully alive, fully human, and completely awake is to be continually thrown out of the nest.
author: Pema Chödrön

text: Each time you stay present with fear and uncertainty, you're letting go of a habitual way of finding security and comfort.
author: Pema Chödrön

text: The problem is that the desire to change is fundamentally a form of aggression toward yourself.
author: Pema Chödrön

text: Try to learn to let what is simply be.
author: Rumi

text: The wound is the place where the Light enters you.
author: Rumi

text: The moment you accept what troubles you've been given, the door will open.
author: Rumi

text: Put your thoughts to sleep, do not let them cast a shadow over the moon of your heart. Let go of thinking.
author: Rumi

text: Let go of your worries and be completely clear-hearted, like the face of a mirror that contains no images.
author: Rumi

text: Yesterday I was clever, so I wanted to change the world. Today I am wise, so I am changing myself.
author: Rumi

text: When you argue with reality, you lose — but only 100% of the time.
author: Byron Katie

text: Letting go gives us freedom, and freedom is the only condition for happiness.
author: Thich Nhat Hanh
source: The Heart of the Buddha's Teaching

text: In the process of letting go you will lose many things from the past, but you will find yourself.
author: Deepak Chopra

text: When I let go of what I am, I become what I might be. When I let go of what I have, I receive what I need.
author: Lao Tzu

text: We must be willing to let go of the life we've planned, so as to have the life that is waiting for us.
author: Joseph Campbell

text: Do everything with a mind that lets go. If you let go a little you will have a little peace; if you let go a lot you will have a lot of peace; if you let go completely you will have complete peace.
author: Ajahn Chah

text: The moment you stop resisting is the moment you allow everything you've been seeking to find you.
author: Michael Singer

text: Feel the feeling but don't become the emotion. Witness it. Allow it. Release it.
author: Crystal Andrus

text: Something amazing happens when we surrender and just love. We melt into another world, a realm of power already within us.
author: Marianne Williamson

text: Six Words of Advice: Let go of what has passed. Let go of what may come. Let go of what is happening now. Don't try to figure anything out. Don't try to make anything happen. Relax, right now, and rest.
author: Tilopa
